topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy printout lay crumpled on the kitchen counter, a stark white expanse marred by vibrant, undeniable lines. Mark stared at the results, his jaw tight. He picked up the plastic vial, turning it over in his fingers as if it might reveal some hidden deception. His brow furrowed deeply, the muscle ticking visibly beneath his skin. "Seriously?" he muttered, the sound sharp and brittle in the quiet morning. He tossed the paper onto the recycling bin with unnecessary force, the clatter echoing in the otherwise silent apartment. He needed coffee, black, strong enough to burn through the sheer absurdity of the revelation.

[story 2]

Sarah paced the length of her living room, her footsteps quick and uneven on the hardwood floor. Each step seemed to carry a weight of frustration. She stopped abruptly, running a hand through her already messy hair, pulling at a strand as if to physically unravel the situation. "This is ridiculous," she sighed, the air leaving her lungs in a frustrated puff. She glared at the computer screen displaying the genetic report, her eyes narrowing to slits. She slammed her palm flat on the desk, the sound loud enough to make the framed photos rattle on the shelf. She needed a minute, a complete break from the sheer weight of the unexpected data.

[story 3]

Liam sat rigidly on the edge of the sofa, meticulously folding and refolding a napkin into a perfect square. His movements were precise, bordering on obsessive, a physical manifestation of his internal turmoil. He kept glancing at the DNA report, his gaze darting back and forth, unable to settle. A low, almost inaudible hum of irritation vibrated in his chest. "Unbelievable," he finally stated, his voice flat and devoid of inflection. He pushed the report away, leaning back until the cushions dipped beneath him, a clear signal that he was done engaging with the topic for the moment. He just wanted the noise to stop.
